Acc@QA.definition: predicted MC answer == gold answer index

IoP.definition: intersection(pred,gold)/duration(pred), max over gold spans, per-question

mIoP.definition: mean of IoP over the evaluated question set

IoU.definition: intersection(pred,gold)/union(pred,gold), max over gold spans, per-question

mIoU.definition: mean of IoU over the evaluated question set

MRR.definition: mean reciprocal rank of first retrieved timestamp inside a gold span

Peak-in-gold.definition: whether the top-1 admitted/retrieved timestamp falls inside a gold span

Candidate-contains-gold.definition: whether the full retrieved candidate set's min/max envelope contains a gold span

Candidate-to-peak_loss.definition: diagnostic distance between best-in-window candidate and the highest-scored admitted frame

Survivor_coverage_ceiling.definition: upper bound on achievable IoP/IoU given only the L1-survivor frame set (best case if L2/L3 were perfect)

Window_coverage.definition: fraction of gold-span duration covered by retrieved timestamps' bounding window

frames_in_window (eval/grounding_scorer..definition: existing repo diagnostic: fraction of retrieved timestamps landing inside any gold span -- pre-dates this setup, kept as-is, explicitly not the official metric set

official_source_of_truth: IoP/IoU/Acc@QA/Acc@GQA definitions as stated in the NExT-GQA paper (Xiao et al., CVPR 2024); multi-gold-span protocol: score against each gold span independently and take the maximum, never concatenate spans -- implemented in scripts/nextgqa_metrics.py::best_over_gold_spans
synthetic_test_status: 29/29 PASS (was 27/27; +2 subcases added during the Part 2c fix below), see test_nextgqa_metrics_synthetic.py -- no real dataset prediction was scored
note_existing_iop_implementation: eval/grounding_scorer.py::iop() computes IoP against the UNION of all gold spans in one prediction interval (single min/max predicted span), which differs from the official max-over-spans protocol used here when a question has multiple disjoint gold spans. This discrepancy is flagged for reconciliation before any official-scale run; not fixed in this setup task since eval/grounding_scorer.py is production code, out of scope for SETUP ONLY.
correction_2026-07-21_part2c_metric_pari: iop_single() previously returned 0.0 for ANY non-positive-length predicted span, including an exactly-zero-width predicted point (pred_s==pred_e) landing inside the gold span. Empirically diffing this module against an independent second implementation (eval/metrics.py) across 29,535 real val_tune (predicted_span, gold_span) pairs and 9 synthetic edge cases found 0 real-data mismatches but 1 synthetic mismatch on exactly this case. Cross-checked against the actual official doc-doc/NExT-GQA scorer (code/TempGQA/eval_ground.py::get_tIoU), which special-cases this exact scenario as IoP=1.0 (perfect precision), IoU=0.0 -- this module was NOT matching the true official formula for that case, despite being labeled canonical/validated. Fixed in iop_single() (see inline comment there); the corresponding test's previously-hardcoded expectation (empty_prediction_iop == 0.0) was itself wrong and has been corrected to match the official spec, not just the new code. This finding never affected any published benchmark number: the zero-duration-predicted-span condition never occurred in the real val_tune retrieval data (l2_retrieve_top_k>=5 makes a single-unique-timestamp retrieval effectively impossible). Full writeup: metric_parity_report.md, metric_parity_diff.csv, metric_parity_synthetic.csv at repo root. As of this correction, scripts/part3_tune.py (the Family 1-5 hyperparameter tuning harness) imports this module directly instead of maintaining a separate reimplementation -- eval/metrics.py's IoP/IoU functions are retired from that harness (predicted_span_from_frames(), which is IRIS-specific and not a duplicate of an official metric, remains in eval/metrics.py).
